channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Justin Bieber Serenades Billie Eilish, Duets With SZA During Guest-Filled Coachella Weekend 2 Set
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to this week's music update. We're coming to you live from the Coachella festival in Indio, California. Weekend two just wrapped up, and boy, did it deliver. Let's dive into the highlights."
[Music transitions to a clip of Justin Bieber's song]
Host: "Justin Bieber took the stage for his second headlining performance, and it was a night to remember. He brought out a host of special guests, including the one and only Billie Eilish. The two performed a rendition of Bieber's 2009 track 'One Less Lonely Girl', and it was clear they had a blast on stage."
[Music transitions to a clip of Billie Eilish speaking]
Host: "Eilish has spoken publicly about her friendship with Bieber, saying he's been a supportive figure in her career. And it shows - the two shared a heartfelt moment on stage, with Bieber even serenading Eilish and sitting her on a stool during the performance."
[Music transitions to a clip of SZA's song]
Host: "But that wasn't the only surprise of the night. Bieber also welcomed SZA for an acoustic duet of her 2022 track 'Snooze', as well as Sexyy Red for 'Sweet Spot'. And if that wasn't enough, he brought out Big Sean for performances of 'As Long As You Love Me' and 'No Pressure'."
[Music transitions to a clip of Justin Bieber's song]
Host: "This performance comes on the heels of Bieber's biggest streaming day of 2026, with 24.6 million streams on Sunday, April 12. That's his highest total since July 2025, according to Luminate. And with a setlist that included songs from his 2025 Swag album, as well as some throwbacks to his early YouTube-era hits, it's no wonder fans are loving it."
[Outro music plays]
Host: "That's all for this week's update. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you in the next one."
